nameMeanings.name: Hills
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hill
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

historicEvents.title: Gregorian Calendar is Adopted
historicEvents.description: Gregorian calendar was adopted in England in 1752. That year, Wednesday, September 2, 1752, was followed by Thursday, September 14th, 1752, which caused the country to skip ahead eleven days.

historicEvents.title: Seven Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Seven Years' War began as a North American conflict then stretched between England and France. England, along with allies, battled France in America, India, and Europe, making it arguably the first global war. The conflict ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1763 and England was victorious. The Seven Years' war ultimately led to discontent in the colonies and the American Revolution.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Hills was born in 1677, her father, John Hill, was 23 and her mother, Mary Ann Fyson, was 22. She had at least 1 son with William Bradley. She died in 1765, at the age of 88, and was buried in Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Hills est née en 1677, son père, John Hill, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Mary Ann Fyson, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec William Bradley. Elle est décédée en 1765, à l’âge de 88 ans, et a été enterrée à Hampshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Hills 1677 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Hill 23 und ihre Mutter Mary Ann Fyson 22. Sie hatte mit William Bradley mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1765 im Alter von 88 Jahren und wurde in Hampshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Hills è nata il nel 1677, sua padre, John Hill, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Mary Ann Fyson, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con William Bradley. Morì nel 1765, all'età di 88 anni, e fu sepolta a Hampshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary Hillsが1677年に生まれた時，父John Hillは23歳，母Mary Ann Fysonは22歳であった。彼女はWilliam Bradleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1765年に88歳で死去し，イギリスハンプシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Hills(이)가 1677년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Hill(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Ann Fyson(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 William Bradley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1765년 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 함프실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Hills nasceu em 1677, seu pai, John Hill, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Mary Ann Fyson, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com William Bradley. Ela faleceu em 1765, com 88 anos, e foi sepultada em Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary Hills родилась в 1677 г., её отцу по имени John Hill было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Ann Fyson, 22. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от William Bradley. Она умерла в 1765 г. в возрасте 88 лет и была похоронена в местности хампшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary Hills nació en 1677, su papá, John Hill, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Mary Ann Fyson, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con William Bradley. Murió en 1765, a la edad de 88 años, y fue sepultada en Hampshire, Inglaterra.
